assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can receive email when the selected route requires it.
No platform account, tenant, credentials, or product resources exist yet.
Developer has a web application environment ready to integrate JavaScript.
Developer has basic knowledge of JavaScript and web development.

developer_goal: Send and receive real-time events in a web application using Pusher Channels.

first_success.outcome: Developer successfully receives and handles a real-time event in their web application using Pusher Channels.
first_success.observable_signal: The bound event handler function executes in the web app, e.g., an alert or console log triggered by the received event.

route_selection.rule: Reconstruct complete official candidate routes, prefer no payment/admin/approval when possible, then minimize required actions; leave consequential ties unresolved.

candidates.name: Pusher Channels Android Client Quick Start
candidates.reason: Present in the machine draft; not selected by an independent shortest-path audit.

candidates.name: Pusher Channels Android Client
candidates.reason: Android quick start is platform-specific and less universal than the JavaScript client which runs in browsers and is the first documented example.

candidates.name: Pusher Beams Push Notifications
candidates.reason: Focused on push notifications setup and requires additional platform-specific setup (e.g., Apple developer account), thus more complex and not the primary first-mile onboarding for Channels.

required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.observable_result: API keys are available in dashboard.

required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.action: Trigger an event from the server using server SDK with app credentials.

required_fields.label: API keys, channel name, event name, and event data.
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.action: Observe the event handler executing in the web app upon receiving the triggered event.

required_fields.label: Triggered event from server.
required_fields.notes: Mapped from machine-drafted primary_path; field inventory not independently audited.

required_path.observable_result: Visible or logged event data in client app.

prerequisites.description: Create a free Pusher account and create a Channels app to obtain API keys (app_id, key, secret, cluster).
Have a web application environment capable of including JavaScript and running client-side code.

external_gates.description: Developer must create a Pusher account and Channels app to obtain API keys.
Developer must include the Pusher client library in their app (JavaScript or Android).
Server SDK requires valid app credentials (app_id, key, secret, cluster) to trigger events.

platform_outcomes.description: Receiving a triggered event in the web app after subscribing and binding to a channel and event.

uncertainties.question: Is there an explicitly documented time-to-first-success claim by Pusher for Channels or Beams?
uncertainties.impact: Without a vendor-claimed time, we cannot report a precise time-to-first-success metric.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: No explicit timing or duration claims found in official documentation or tutorials.

uncertainties.question: Is the first success milestone the same for Pusher Beams as for Channels?
uncertainties.impact: Different products may have different onboarding flows and first success definitions.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Pusher Beams documentation focuses on instance creation and notification sending but does not clearly define a first success milestone comparable to Channels.

uncertainties.question: Has the machine-drafted Pusher route been independently shortest-path audited?
uncertainties.impact: Action counts and peer comparison must stay withheld until a human audit passes.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Complete a shortest-path audit against current official docs and mark verified only when evidence supports it.
